title: INTENTION payload
description: Emitted before any tool call or major step. Declares what the agent is about to do, what it touches, and whether it requires acknowledgment. The most important message type; drives all conflict detection.

tool.description: Name of the tool/function the agent intends to call.

args.description: Arguments that will be passed to the tool.

description.description: Free-text description for actions that do not map to a single tool call.

action.description: What the agent is about to do. Either a structured tool call or a free-text description.

scope.description: Resources or domains this action touches. Used for scope-overlap conflict detection. Use dotted paths (e.g., 'auth.middleware', 'db.users.schema'). The router queries for active intentions with overlapping scopes.

expected_outcome.description: Brief description of what the agent expects to result from this action. Used by the coordinator to detect divergent execution paths.

estimated_duration_ms.description: Optional. The agent's estimate of how long this action will take. Used for scheduling and for stale-claim cleanup.

blocking.description: If true, the SDK pauses up to 50ms after emitting to wait for CONFLICT or BLOCK signals from peers/coordinator before executing the action.

uncertainty.description: Optional. Things the agent is unsure about. Surfaces ambiguity to peers and coordinator.

blocks_others.description: Optional. Scopes/resources that other agents should not modify until this intention resolves. Stronger claim than 'scope'.
